verses.text: The Lord will remove from Judah every stay — food, water, the mighty man, the judge, the elder. He strips away their props. When God withdraws the supports of a society, it falls into helplessness.
The Lord will remove from Judah every stay — food, water, the mighty man, the judge, the elder. He strips away their props. When God withdraws the supports of a society, it falls into helplessness.
The Lord will remove from Judah every stay — food, water, the mighty man, the judge, the elder. He strips away their props. When God withdraws the supports of a society, it falls into helplessness.

Children and babes shall rule; the people oppress one another, and none will take charge of the ruin. Leadership collapses into chaos. A people under judgment finds no one able or willing to mend its breach.

Children and babes shall rule; the people oppress one another, and none will take charge of the ruin. Leadership collapses into chaos. A people under judgment finds no one able or willing to mend its breach.

Children and babes shall rule; the people oppress one another, and none will take charge of the ruin. Leadership collapses into chaos. A people under judgment finds no one able or willing to mend its breach.

Children and babes shall rule; the people oppress one another, and none will take charge of the ruin. Leadership collapses into chaos. A people under judgment finds no one able or willing to mend its breach.
Jerusalem is ruined because their words and deeds provoke the LORD, and they 'declare their sin as Sodom,' not hiding it. Brazen sin invites ruin. To boast of wickedness is to hasten judgment.
Jerusalem is ruined because their words and deeds provoke the LORD, and they 'declare their sin as Sodom,' not hiding it. Brazen sin invites ruin. To boast of wickedness is to hasten judgment.

'Say ye to the righteous, that it shall be well with him,' but woe to the wicked; God's people are oppressed by children and misled by their guides. Two destinies are pronounced. The righteous fare well even when society is corrupt.

'Say ye to the righteous, that it shall be well with him,' but woe to the wicked; God's people are oppressed by children and misled by their guides. Two destinies are pronounced. The righteous fare well even when society is corrupt.

'Say ye to the righteous, that it shall be well with him,' but woe to the wicked; God's people are oppressed by children and misled by their guides. Two destinies are pronounced. The righteous fare well even when society is corrupt.

The LORD stands to plead and judge, charging the elders, 'ye have eaten up the vineyard... what mean ye that ye beat my people?' Oppression of the poor is on trial. God Himself takes up the cause of the crushed.

The LORD stands to plead and judge, charging the elders, 'ye have eaten up the vineyard... what mean ye that ye beat my people?' Oppression of the poor is on trial. God Himself takes up the cause of the crushed.

The LORD stands to plead and judge, charging the elders, 'ye have eaten up the vineyard... what mean ye that ye beat my people?' Oppression of the poor is on trial. God Himself takes up the cause of the crushed.
The proud daughters of Zion walk mincing and adorned, so the Lord will smite them with disease and shame. Vanity meets humbling. Outward finery cannot shield a heart far from God.
The proud daughters of Zion walk mincing and adorned, so the Lord will smite them with disease and shame. Vanity meets humbling. Outward finery cannot shield a heart far from God.
In that day He will strip away their ornaments — anklets, headbands, crescents, and chains. Their treasured adornments are removed. What pride displays, judgment takes away.
In that day He will strip away their ornaments — anklets, headbands, crescents, and chains. Their treasured adornments are removed. What pride displays, judgment takes away.
In that day He will strip away their ornaments — anklets, headbands, crescents, and chains. Their treasured adornments are removed. What pride displays, judgment takes away.
He will take the rings, bracelets, veils, mirrors, and fine garments. Every trinket of vanity is named. God notices and removes what the proud heart trusts in.
He will take the rings, bracelets, veils, mirrors, and fine garments. Every trinket of vanity is named. God notices and removes what the proud heart trusts in.
He will take the rings, bracelets, veils, mirrors, and fine garments. Every trinket of vanity is named. God notices and removes what the proud heart trusts in.

Instead of perfume there shall be stench, instead of beauty branding; her men shall fall by the sword, and her gates shall mourn. The reversal is complete. The glory of the proud city turns to grief and desolation.
